titleStmt.title: Letter from Peter Mooney to his sister Katie Mooney, 24 January 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Peter Mooney

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p:
Letter from Peter Mooney to his sister Katie. The letter is filled with questions about life at home: the well being of his family, friends and the farm. He writes about his training schedule and about a Mass he attended. He closes by reminiscing on his time at home and writes about how much he misses those at home.
Peter Mooney was born on 5th February 1886 in Folistown, Dunboyne. Orphaned at age 7, Peter and his sister Katie (b. 1884) were brought up by their uncle William and his wife Mary in Caulstown. While there are no surviving enlistment papers for Peter, his War Badge Index Record shows his enlistment date as 18th October 1915.
He served with the Royal Artillery and later transferred to the Royal Engineers where he was promoted to the rank of Sergeant. He served in several places at the Western Front, including Ypres, St.Omer and Passchendael, in Belgium.Peter was an avid correspondent with his sister Kate and this letter is from a collection of over ninety surviving letters.

correspAction.placeName: 5th Reserve Brigade, Royal Field Artillery, Ballincollig, Co. Cork, Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
5th Reserve Brigade R.F.A.
Ballincollig. Jan 24th 1916
Dear Katie. You are indeed very
kind to write to me so
often I am glad to see
by your letters that
things are going on so well at home. I am
doing well & getting the
very best of health.
The weather was very wet
this last few days
Tell her to pray for me
as her prayers should be
heard. The Majors name
here is Major Calvert &
a strict old boy he is
Tell my Aunt she can
keep the money she got
for the heifer all I ask
in return is a few prayers.
Tell me if you want
anything & I will see about
it. I do often think of
you all at home when
evening time comes I think
of the cows that I used
to milk. It will be
Heaven to me when I get
home to you all again.
We all go to 12.00 mass
at the Parish Chapel we have
to march about a quarter of
a mile. We did not get
much drill yet but we
have a lot to do with
horses cleaning them &
walking them out every <lb/day. We are all sleeping on
straw stuffed into linen
bags. We have no sheets
nothing more than three
brown rugs. Still I am
happy & doing well so
far
They seem to have forgotten
me altogether in the
Paddock I wonder why
it is. How is Paddy
I must give him a hand
to dress them sheep when
I get home. Tell Jimmy
Reilly I was asking for
him is Jim Mullally still
working the forge. I
have no more at present
so I say Good Bye &
may God Bless you all
pray for me. Pr. Mooney
11 JUL 16 Miss. K. Mooney
Caulstown
Dunboyne
County Meath
Ireland
